title: Q-1 — Objects are exhibit-nested, not a shared pool
rationale: ADR-0001. Objects + their Notes are owned by the Exhibit (self-contained, independently-portable IIIF Manifest). No Library-level shared object pool, no cross-exhibit reuse. Reverses our own earlier shared-pool principle deliberately for portability. Do not re-propose a shared pool without reweighing portability.

title: Q-2 — Rendering = 3-layer headless core + thin adapters; Svelte everywhere
rationale: ADR-0002. @render/core (pure TS) -> @render/mount (vanilla mount fns: OSD+Annotorious+Wavesurfer) -> thin per-framework adapters (<500 LOC = leak detector). Studio = Svelte SPA; Viewer = Astro+Svelte islands. ONE adapter @render/svelte, zero @render/react. Two questions, one ADR: not independently reversible.

title: Q-3 — Annotation spine = append-only log -> version-DAG merge -> heads/history WADM
rationale: ADR-0003 (keystone). Each Note: {logicalId,version,parent,modifiedAt,lastEditor}. Append-only log (edits bump version, keep parent; deletes=tombstones; ids never reused). Version-parent DAG yields three-way merge WITHOUT git (merge-base = parent-chain walk). modifiedAt is in-card tiebreaker ONLY (clock-skew makes LWW a data-loss bug). Heads-projection (only head versions; plural after unresolved merge = honest degradation) + history sidecar (annotations/history/{logicalId}.json + index.json; id={logicalId}/v{n}; archie:hasHistory + prov:wasRevisionOf). Three-tier interop: pure WADM->current, PROV-aware->history, Archie->full DAG. version+parent written on EVERY edit from day one; retrofitting is the expensive path this avoids.

title: Q-4 — Deep-zoom tiling does NOT use wasm-vips
rationale: ADR-0004. No dzsave binding exists; binary is ~13-20MB (blows budget 50-80x). v1 = single responsive JPEG via OSD type:image (~6000-8000px) + external IIIF info.json escape hatch. v1.1 = OffscreenCanvas DZI slicer, NOT libvips-WASM at any version. Stop the obvious 'just use libvips' suggestion from recurring.

title: Q-5 — Source-before-projection through-line (define authoritative source, project thin)
rationale: CONTEXT.md through-line. Every boundary = authoritative source-of-truth + thin derived projection/adapter. Build source first, projection second, always: log before heads-page; Filesystem seam before backends; original image before display-master; @render/core before mount before adapter; zip before host-adapters. Merge conflict-card is the one interactive/stateful projection (highest impl-risk-per-line). Orphan gaps resist the pattern -> scheduled by GATE not dependency.

name: wadm-types-local-to-core
description: Archie defines structurally-compatible WADM types LOCALLY in @render/core/wadm/types.ts (not re-exported from @annotorious/openseadragon like anvil does) to keep the data-model spine pure/testable without the renderer. Phase 1 must verify structural compat at the W3CImageFormat mount seam (spike-0001 module 2). Branded ids: ADR-0029 phantom-symbol pattern; ULID logicalId + {logicalId}/v{n} grammar. Q-3.

title: Q-6 — Concurrent-head version-id collision: log tolerates, serialization disambiguates (scheme TBD P0-4/P0-6)
rationale: ADR-0003 gap found at P0-3. Two clients editing v1->v2 concurrently both produce {logicalId}/v2 -> the resolvable-path grammar collides under concurrency. RENUMBERING incoming versions is REJECTED: it breaks citation integrity (Alice's cited {logicalId}/v2 would drift to other content), violating the provenance reason append-only was locked for. Decision: (1) P0-3 single-writer-linear invariants enforced by helpers (appendNew/Edit/Delete), NOT a global versionId-uniqueness key on the log type; (2) the log type tolerates colliding (logicalId,version) records assembled directly (this IS plural heads); (3) serialization (P0-6) emits distinct id IRIs only when a collision exists -- earlier/existing record keeps its id, later-arriver gets a deterministic suffix (lastEditor/content-hash), preserving existing citations; (4) linear common case keeps clean {logicalId}/v{n}. Specific disambiguator scheme finalized in P0-4 merge pass.

title: ADR-0003 amended (2026-05-25): separate rev (ULID DAG-node id) from {logicalId}/v{n} citation IRI
rationale: P0-4 found version-id cannot be both citation IRI and DAG parent pointer under concurrency (second-order lineage ambiguous). Record now carries rev:RevId (per-record ULID) as DAG node; parent:RevId|null; version/{logicalId}/v{n} retained as citation projection disambiguated on collision (narrowed Q-6). Rejected content-hash rev (coalesces concurrent identical-content edits). Git's model. See docs/adr/0003 Refinement section.

name: phase-0-interop-gate-passed
description: ADR-0003 three-tier interop contract PROVEN at unit-test level (P0-7, the Phase-0 gate). A simulated pure WADM consumer loading only the heads page (cannot follow archie:hasHistory) renders exactly one overlay per note (the head), zero history-version leak; plural heads on unresolved concurrent merge = honest degradation (both shown, distinct ids via Q-6); tombstoned notes disappear. The real third-party-viewer (Mirador/UV) test remains the eventual proof; this unit surrogate is the Phase-0 gate. packages/render-core/src/spine/interop.test.ts.

name: phase-0-complete
description: Phase 0 (skeleton + data-model spine) COMPLETE 2026-05-25. pnpm monorepo (@render/core|mount|svelte + apps studio|viewer); 96 tests green, typecheck clean. Keystone spine done: append-only log -> version-DAG merge (rev-based) -> heads projection -> heads/history WADM serialization; THE GATE (pure-WADM-consumer interop) green. Q-1..Q-6 minted; ADR-0003 amended (rev vs version-id). DEFERRED obligations carried forward: (1) real bundle measurement is a Phase-2 gate (strategy §33; 240KB never validated, treat as measurement-triggers-action not a number); (2) EXIF fixture binaries + consumer wired at orphan-gate; (3) WADM structural-compat with Annotorious W3CImageFormat verified at Phase-1 mount seam; (4) Q-6 publish-history-aware citation preservation is future. Next: Phase 1 extraction (characterization-testing first).

content: WADM @context appears ONLY at the AnnotationPage level, never on individual Annotation items (Q-3 'context never mixed'). recordToAnnotation defaults withContext=false; toHeadsPage/toHistory set @context on the page only. A pure consumer reads one context per resource. Do not add @context to per-item annotations.

name: phase-1-gate-logic-proven-visual-owed
description: Phase-1 gate (spike-0001 acceptance): the new @render/mount fitBounds-on-select path is proven HEADLESS to match the anvil-stock characterization oracle — dispatchFitBounds produces the SAME rect as fitBoundsRect for rect+polygon+sidebar-expansion (gate.test.ts), and CanvasController binds selection correctly incl. the inversion (controller.test.ts). NOT yet verified: a real OSD viewer fitting visually + anvil-stock side-by-side in a browser (anvil has no fitBounds test; needs a real DOM/canvas). That visual equivalence is a HUMAN verification step (memory: lightpanda wedges here; happy-dom only imports, doesn't render). Honest scope — logic gate green, visual gate owed.

name: phase-2-source-spine-complete
description: Phase 2 SOURCE side complete 2026-05-25 (144 tests workspace-wide). @render/core now has: annotation persistence round-trip over the Filesystem seam (deserialize+persist; archie: DAG meta on history annotations); IIIF projections (toManifest/toCollection/toExhibitsJson + shouldRenderGallery, UX-Q7); storage backends (MemoryFilesystem, ZipFilesystem=DownloadFilesystem core via fflate, FsaFilesystem=Chromium folder typechecked); publish primitive (publishLibrary + libraryToZip, capstone integration). REMAINING Phase-2 = UI/dogfood (browser/human, seed Archie-79be). Other v1 pure features still buildable headless: VTT->WADM transcript adapter, tag/layer filtering, body sanitization, EXIF orientation mapping, schema migration runner (orphan gaps).

title: Q-7 — Merge resolution needs multi-parent merge nodes (parent -> parents); defer with conflict-card UI
rationale: P0-4 built conflict DETECTION (classifyMerge). RESOLUTION (collapsing plural heads to one) requires a merge node with >=2 parents (git-style merge commit): a single-parent resolution leaves the other concurrent head dangling as a second head. This means extending AnnotationRecord.parent (RevId|null) to parents (RevId[]) across log/merge/heads/serialize/deserialize. DEFERRED: the conflict-card UI that DRIVES resolution is Phase 3 (human-gated, strategy §83), so the multi-parent model is best designed alongside it, not speculatively now. headsOf already returns the plural set correctly; resolution is the missing apply-step. Build when Phase 3 conflict-card work starts.

name: v1-data-logic-layer-complete
description: 2026-05-25: @render/core holds the complete machine-verifiable v1 data/logic layer (168 tests workspace-wide, typecheck clean). Covers: spine (log/DAG/heads/serialize/deserialize/interop-gate), extraction (mount+svelte fitBounds gate), persistence (seam round-trip), IIIF (manifest/collection/exhibits.json), storage backends (memory/zip/fsa), publish (libraryToZip), AV transcripts (VTT/SRT), layer/tag filtering, schema migration runner (§39), linkability resolution. Q-1..Q-7. REMAINING is browser/human only: Phase-2 UI (Studio/Viewer Svelte/Astro), dogfood+bundle measurement, Phase-3 comprehension gates (human §83), Phase-1 visual check, render-layer bits (sanitization/EXIF-pixel/empty-states/contrast), merge resolution (Q-7 multi-parent, with conflict-card UI). Continuing autonomously = unverifiable UI = reward-hacking risk (§89).

name: bundle-measurement-renderer-floor
description: Bundle measurement (docs/bundle-size.json, esbuild+gzip): @render/core data-layer = 7.8KB gz (excl fflate), 13.8KB gz (incl fflate) — tiny. OSD+Annotorious+plugin-tools renderer floor = 222.7KB gz (790KB min), NO tree-shaking. CONFIRMS strategy §150: the renderer floor alone (~223KB) is at the 240KB budget BEFORE any Archie/UI code; the budget concern is the renderer, NOT our code. §33 implication: the eventual app bundle (renderer + Svelte/Astro islands + UI) will exceed 240KB — likely within ~2x (ship+document) but the PRECISE built-app number is the Phase-2 dogfood (human). 240KB was never realistic for an OSD+Annotorious product (§150 'do not cite as binding').

toManifest set canvas.annotations[0].id = {canvasId}/annotations(.json) but publishLibrary wrote ONE heads.json per EXHIBIT, not per-canvas -> manifest pointed at non-existent files; a pure consumer 404s. Isolated unit tests (P0-7 heads page in isolation) missed it; only a collection->manifest->canvas->file traversal test caught it (advisor-flagged).
resolution: publishLibrary now writes per-canvas heads pages at {slug}/canvas/{objId}/annotations.json grouped by target.source; manifest id given .json; added headsPageFromRecords + targetSource to serialize. Phase-2 interop gate test (publish/interop.test.ts) traverses end-to-end. LESSON: cross-module URL linkage needs an integration test, not just per-module unit tests.

description: MemoryFilesystem persisted empty directories; ZipFilesystem (dirs implicit as path prefixes) only shows a dir once it has content. The conformance suite caught it.
resolution: Contract pinned in seam.ts: empty-dir persistence is NOT guaranteed; a dir is observable in entries() once it has content. Conformance test reuses the dir handle (the real usage pattern). Both backends now conform.

name: studio-demo-real-bundle-287kb-gz
description: Studio verification demo built (apps/studio, vite build OK): real app bundle = 981KB min / 287KB gz (OSD+Annotorious+Svelte+Archie+fflate), CSS 9.5KB/2.2KB gz. §33 datapoint: ~1.2x of the 240KB budget -> 'within ~2x: ship + document the real figure' tier. Demo exercises full stack: spine(log->heads)->@render/svelte->@render/mount(real OSD+Annotorious)->fitBounds-on-select, + layer filter + libraryToZip download. Added to mount: setAnnotations(W3CAnnotation[]) + canvasId option (annotation target identity distinct from loaded image). resolve.ts now accepts .svg. 199 tests green.

name: studio-editor-built
description: Studio editor built (browser-verify pending). Real annotate loop over headless-tested AnnotationSession (@render/core/session): draw rect/polygon -> createNote -> WADM form (comment/tags/layers) -> edit/delete -> layer filter -> fitBounds-on-select -> publish .archie.zip. Mount contract extended: drawing + onCreate/onUpdate/onDelete + setAnnotations + canvasId. 204 tests, typecheck clean, vite build OK 290KB gz.

name: viewer-app-built
description: Viewer app built (Astro + Svelte islands, browser-verify pending). apps/viewer: Reader.svelte island = read-only OSD Canvas + annomea-style 3-state pane (list<->detail) + popup on marker select; reads the published heads-page form (toHeadsPage). index.astro hosts it client:only='svelte' (OSD touches document at import -> can't SSR -> client-only is the Astro idiom). astro build OK (1 page, 267KB gz island). Both surfaces now build+run: Studio editor :5173, Viewer :4321.

name: body-sanitization-built
description: Body sanitization (CONTEXT named-cut §151 XSS gate) built + tested. @render/svelte/sanitize.ts sanitizeHtml() via isomorphic-dompurify (native in browser, jsdom in node -> headless-testable): strips script/event-handlers/javascript: URLs, keeps safe markup. 6 tests. Render layer uses it for HTML/markdown note bodies. The named-cut gate 'before first user-authored HTML' is now closeable.

title: Q-7 RESOLVED (logic): merge resolution via additive mergeParents (not parent->parents)
rationale: Built resolveConflict (merge.ts): appends a multi-parent merge node collapsing plural heads to one. Chose ADDITIVE mergeParents?: RevId[] alongside parent (less invasive than the parent->parents rewrite the advisor floated): parent=lexicographically-first head, mergeParents=rest; version=max+1. headsOf counts mergeParents as references; ancestors() now multi-parent BFS; round-trips via archie:mergeParents. 4 tests. The version-DAG spine is complete (P0-4 detect + Q-7 resolve). REMAINING: only the conflict-card UI that DRIVES resolveConflict (Phase-3, browser/human).

name: ui-applies-curators-study-system
description: Studio + Viewer UI restyled to the established design system (.interface-design/system.md 'curator's study at night') — replaced generic system-ui/gray/blue. Canonical tokens in apps/{studio,viewer}/src/tokens.css: dark light-table canvas + warm-paper panels + vermillion accent; Cormorant Garamond/Crimson Text/Work Sans/JetBrains Mono (Google Fonts); 4px spacing; surface-shift depth. Both build clean (studio vite, viewer astro). Per user: apply interface-design whenever building Archie UI (memory: apply-interface-design-for-archie-ui).

name: viewer-gallery-landing-built
description: Viewer Gallery landing built (locked frame 'exhibit gallery'; CONTEXT Gallery/UX-Q7). index.astro = static gallery (no island, SSG-safe): warm gallery-wall surface, exhibit cards (cover+title+desc+count), Cormorant titles, invite-in styling per system.md. Reader moved to /sample. 2 pages build clean. As-built component patterns saved to .interface-design/system.md (185 lines).

name: studio-live-persistence-opfs
description: Studio editor now persists to OPFS (working store, UX-Q1/Q2). apps/studio/src/store.ts openAnnotationsDir() = FsaFilesystem(navigator.storage.getDirectory()) -> no new backend (OPFS root IS a FileSystemDirectoryHandle). App.svelte: load-on-mount (restore persisted work else seed+save), autosave (800ms debounce on edit), Save button + dirty indicator (vermillion dot) in the light-table header. session is $state (reassign on load) + rev bump for mutation reactivity. Browser-verify: edit -> reload -> notes persist. 214 tests, builds clean.

name: studio-merge-ui-prototype
description: Merge/collaboration UI prototype built (CONTEXT #1 validation-priority invention; HUMAN-GATED §83 for comprehension). AnnotationSession gained importChanges/conflicts/conflictHeads/resolve (headless-tested, 6 session tests) — the spine's mergeLogs+headsOf already make ff=single-head, concurrent=plural-heads; resolve wraps resolveConflict (Q-7). apps/studio/MergeReview.svelte: 'Import changes' simulates a colleague's concurrent edit -> summary panel ('Synced N · M need your decision · Review/Later') -> conflict cards (both sides, keep-one) -> resolve. Design-system styled. 215 tests, builds clean. The conflict-card UI (Q-7's remaining UI half) is now DONE; only the comprehension gate (user-run) remains.

name: ui-empty-loading-error-states
description: Empty/loading/error states added (orphan gate §39 'before public Viewer ships'; system.md §Reader States). Canvas.svelte (shared): loading overlay (pulsing vermillion dot) until OSD opens, error overlay on open-failed (try/catch around createMount). Editor + Reader: empty states ('No notes yet — draw to begin' / layer-filter-empty / 'No notes on this object'). Design-system styled (dashed paper border, light-table overlay). Both apps build clean.

name: narrative-sections-ranges + draw-racefix
description: (1) Narrative layout data foundation: model.ts Section {id,title,objectId,xywh?,prose?} + Exhibit.sections?; iiif toRanges() projects to IIIF Ranges (start = active canvas/region); toManifest emits structures[] when sections exist. Pure, 2 tests, 187 core. The prose-pane UI remains (browser). (2) Draw bug: applied a mount-race robustness fix in Canvas.svelte (apply drawing state at end of onMount, since the $effects only re-run on tool/drawing CHANGE and surface is assigned async) — NOT confirmed as the root cause; needs browser console verify (HANDOFF has steps).

name: layout-resolver
description: resolveLayout (model/layout.ts) + Exhibit.layout?:LayoutType (single|grid|narrative). Author's explicit choice wins; else infer (sections->narrative, >1 object->grid, else single). Pure, 4 tests, 191 core. v1 layout set now DATA-complete (resolution + Section->Range). Remaining is layout UI rendering (browser): grid thumbnails, narrative prose-pane.

name: ghpages-publish-adapter
description: GH-Pages publish adapter (publish/ghpages.ts). PURE+tested: collectFiles (walk seam -> path->content map) + buildGitTree (GitHub git-trees blob payload, replace-this-tree). publishToGitHub (createTree->commit->updateRef) sketched = browser/network layer. 223 tests workspace, typecheck clean. The publish story (zip primitive + GH adapter) is now logic-complete; only the 'Connect to GitHub' token-paste UI remains (browser).

Studio App crashed on mount (Svelte each_key_duplicate) — the notes {#each ... (r.logicalId)} key collided because projectHeads returns TWO records sharing a logicalId when an unresolved conflict (plural heads, from a prior Import) was restored from OPFS. The crash aborted the whole App, so draw + everything appeared broken. The console error (each_key_duplicate, logicalId at App.svelte:148/onMount) pinned it instantly — raw data > blind guessing.
resolution: Key the notes each-block by r.rev (unique per record), not r.logicalId. LESSON: under plural heads (a v1 reality), NEVER key/id UI by logicalId — use rev. Same risk in workingAnnotations (id=logicalId) -> Annotorious duplicate id when a conflict exists; consider rev there too. Builds clean; user to re-verify draw (crash was the blocker).

title: Phase-2 layouts = Single+Grid only; Narrative is Phase-3
rationale: Strategy §27 scopes Phase 2 to Single+Grid (object-led reading). §31/§36 list Narrative + overview-as-canvas as Phase-3 invented tier requiring human comprehension gates. HANDOFF had drifted (lumped Narrative into Phase-2 remaining UI); corrected.

name: one-log-per-exhibit-objects-via-target-source
description: Multi-object Studio: the spine is ONE annotation log per EXHIBIT, not per object. Each note's target.source IS its object's canvas id (BASE/slug/canvas/objId) — exactly how publishLibrary groups heads per canvas. A multi-object editor needs NO per-object AnnotationSession and NO per-object OPFS dir: one session holds the whole exhibit, the editor shows the current object and filters notes by srcOf(target)===canvasId. Object switcher = view state only. Do NOT introduce per-object sessions.

Canvas.svelte (render-svelte) reads its source prop ONLY in onMount (createMount); there is NO effect watching source. So changing source on a mounted Canvas does NOT reload the OSD image — new annotations render over the OLD image. Found 2026-05-25 in the multi-object Studio rail.
resolution: Any UI that switches the displayed object on a persistent Canvas MUST remount via {#key canvasId} ... {/key}, OR give Canvas.svelte a source effect that destroys+recreates the surface. Studio now wraps its Canvas in {#key canvasId}.

description: resolveTileSource (render-core iiif/resolve.ts) falls through to "treat as IIIF service base + append /info.json" for any source without a known image extension. An OPFS-imported file resolved to a blob: URL has no extension → OSD fetched blob:.../info.json → Security Error / HTTP 0, image never rendered (P2-7).
resolution: blob: and data: URLs carry image bytes directly and are never IIIF — classify them as {kind:"image"} at the top of resolveTileSource (before the extension/fallback checks). +2 regression tests. NOTE: extensionless http(s) URLs are still treated as IIIF bases by design.

name: github-publish-binary-as-base64-blobs
description: Publishing binary (imported images) to GitHub Pages via the git-trees API: git-trees inline `content` is UTF-8 only, so binary files must be uploaded as blobs first — POST /git/blobs {content: <base64>, encoding: "base64"} → returns sha → reference it in the tree entry as {path, mode, type:"blob", sha} (not content). Text files (JSON) stay inline {content}. collectFiles classifies by extension (FileContent = {text}|{base64}); chunked btoa avoids call-stack blowups on large images. publishLibrary writes asset bytes via getAsset(slug,name) + rewrites the canvas image URL to {slug}/assets/{name}.

title: Q-1 — Intra-Library link refs: archie: in body, resolved on heads projection, raw in history
rationale: The in-body link ref is an archie: URI encoding a LinkTarget (one source of truth = the markdown body, per ADR-0003 log->projection). It is rewritten to a real display URL ONLY on the consumer heads-page projection at publish (site.ts rewriteArchieLinks before headsPageFromRecords); the history sidecar is NEVER rewritten because loadLibrary/readAnnotations reconstructs the canonical log from history (persist.ts), so rewriting it would lose the structured ref on Open-zip->edit->republish. Accepted cost: a foreign PROV-aware consumer reading raw history sees archie: link-text (DOMPurify strips the unknown scheme) = honest degradation per CONTEXT 85; it could not resolve an intra-Library ref regardless. DOMPurify html-profile strips non-standard schemes -> the rewrite MUST run before any sanitize. publishLibrary returns {brokenLinks}; broken/malformed refs degrade to plain text. Implements CONTEXT 95.

title: Q-1 — AV hand-annotation is v1, not just VTT/SRT import
rationale: User ruling 2026-05-25 (supersedes CONTEXT 81 'AV transcripts = import-only v1'): the Studio edit side must let an author annotate audio/video BY HAND — mark a time region on a player and attach a note — not only import WebVTT/SRT. Same authority pattern as the 'phase 3 is part of v1' ruling. Chosen interaction model: mark-in / mark-out (capture currentTime as start, then end) -> createNote at t=start,end -> WADM form (the temporal analogue of the OSD rect draw tool); WADM form gains editable start/end time fields for AV notes. File INGEST (upload/codec/size) stays gated per 152; this ruling is about the annotation interaction, against an existing AV source.

title: Q-2 — Layout = two orthogonal axes (CONTEXT 43): arrangement (LayoutType) + reading mode (mode field)
rationale: Finalized 2026-05-25 (user gate). The layout-picker first read as interchangeable templates; fix per CONTEXT 43 = model layout as TWO axes. SPATIAL ARRANGEMENT = LayoutType (single|grid|narrative; a NEW arrangement like compare in v1.1 is a new LayoutType value). READING MODE = Exhibit.mode?/ExhibitMeta.mode? (string, reserved; a new pacing mode of an existing arrangement — slideshow of grid, scrollytelling of narrative per 92 — attaches via mode, NOT a new LayoutType). readingFamily(layout)->object-led|prose-led groups the picker by the reading-mode axis; isValidMode(layout,mode) encodes the family-binding rule (v1: only undefined valid; v1.1 widens). mode unused in v1 (no published-file change, no migration). Picker (LayoutPicker.svelte) groups options by family + shows where v1.1 modes attach, so it cannot sprawl into a flat template menu. Chose string+named-values over a never-discriminator for JSON round-trip simplicity (advisor-offered alternative).

title: Q-1 — Playground vs Project is PER-EXHIBIT (example=template/playground, user-created=project), not a session mode
rationale: User gate 2026-05-25 (rejected the session-wide two-door first cut as 'same examples in both buckets, incoherent paths'). MODEL: isTemplate(slug)=slug in DEFAULT_EXHIBITS. A bundled EXAMPLE is a template -> playground: opening shows the amber 'Example - not saved, Keep a copy' banner; save() no-ops + openExhibit seeds fresh (notes never persist). A USER-CREATED exhibit = project: saved, no banner. LibraryHome badges examples. keepCopy() forks the current example into a new saved user exhibit, carrying current head notes retargeted to the copy's canvas IRIs (fresh records). Library STRUCTURE always persists (persistLibrary ungated); only example annotations are ephemeral (save gated on isTemplate(currentSlug)). Each example has ONE role + ONE in/out path. Landing.svelte + session appMode + convertToProject DELETED. §116 three-configs persistence (Chromium folder vs zip-as-file) remains a separate gated invention.

title: Q-2 — Three-configs persistence as one Project (invention #3)
rationale: A Library's canonical bytes live in one of three places, capability-selected, capability HIDDEN: unbound = OPFS this-browser-only; folder = Chromium FsaFilesystem autosave-in-place (publishLibrary writes the published IIIF tree = the git/GH-Pages on-ramp); file = a .archie.zip on disk (Save downloads via libraryToZip, Open picks it — the Word-doc-2003 model). ADAPTATION of CONTEXT $115/$116: the per-exhibit Example model (Q-1) already carries the ephemeral-playground half, so the user-facing axis is unbound-vs-bound (NOT the rejected two-door Playground/Project Landing, which stays deleted); binding chrome lives on LibraryHome. Pure model + recents algebra in @render/core fs/binding.ts (13 tests); browser glue (showDirectoryPicker, IndexedDB handle store, localStorage recents+lastBinding) in apps/studio binding.ts + handles-db.ts; NO new persistence plumbing (reuses publishLibrary/loadLibrary/libraryToZip/openZip). Folder autosave = write-through the tree on save(); purist OPFS-as-irrelevant deferred to v1.1. GATED: prototype built, STOP for user comprehension test; browser-verify owed (FSA/IndexedDB/localStorage headless-unverifiable). 290 tests green, both apps build.

title: Q-1 — Exhibit overview-as-canvas (invention #1)
rationale: Opening a multi-object or narrative exhibit lands on an OVERVIEW scale: its Objects as plates on a pannable/zoomable dark tableau in reading order — the 1a 'same zoom metaphor' (wheel zoom-to-cursor + drag-pan via a CSS transform on the tableau), click a plate → openObject → the deep-zoom editor; back climbs to the overview. Single-object exhibits skip straight to the editor (overview trivial). 1b fallback = an explicit List view toggle inside the SAME component (the contrast the gate measures against). New view scale view='overview' between library and editor; hasOverview = >1 object OR narrative. ExhibitOverview.svelte built SELF-CONTAINED/additive FIRST, wired second, so a mid-build interruption could never break the working open-exhibit flow. Section dividers for narrative DEFERRED (no Studio section-authoring yet → no section data). GATED: prototype built, STOP for the 'does it feel like a canvas, not a list?' comprehension test; browser-verify owed (pointer/wheel transforms are headless-unverifiable). 290 tests green; Studio builds (188 modules). All 6 v1 inventions are now built.

title: Q-1 — Section = self-contained third layer, not a tour of Notes
rationale: Grilled via /grill-with-docs (2026-05-25). FORK RESOLVED: (A) third layer OVER (B) tour-of-notes. A Section = { objectId, start, prose } — its own camera+prose beat, INDEPENDENT of Notes. (A) chosen because: CONTEXT already defines start as 'what the canvas shows' (not a Note ref); (A) subsumes (B) (a note-walk = sections whose start=the note's region + prose ⌘K-citing it); multi-object + AV narratives fall out for free; (B) welds the narrative layer to the annotation layer and can't focus a non-Note region. start = a MEDIA FRAGMENT (xywh=… for image objects, t=start,end for AV — same grammar as Note selectors), so Section.xywh is REPLACED by Section.start (full fragment). The spine may SWITCH objects across sections; single-object is the degenerate (all sections share objectId). Note cross-refs = ⌘K links in prose, NOT a structural field. Reading: show the ACTIVE object's markers; §122 progressive-reveal DEFERRED to v1.1 AND redefined under (A) as 'markers whose region the narrative has passed' (the old 'with their section' definition died with B). Editor affordance 'add section from a Note' (seeds start+prose from a Note) gives B's convenience inside A. IMPLEMENTATION (the incoherence to fix — 3 models in 3 places today): NarrativeEditor=A, bidar fixture=B, Viewer NarrativeReader=B-by-index. Fix: (1) NarrativeReader drives the canvas from the ACTIVE section's resolved target (switch object on objectId change, fitBounds the start fragment) — delete the section-i↔note-i index coupling; (2) Section.xywh→Section.start (full fragment); toRanges does canvasId#${start}; sectionsFromManifest parses any fragment; (3) bidar fixture sections carry explicit start (lifted from each note's region); (4) NarrativeEditor offers a temporal start when the bound object is AV + an 'add from note' shortcut. CONTEXT §Language Section sharpened inline.

title: Q-2 — Published artifact is self-describing: static per-exhibit HTML with per-note anchors (durable refs)
rationale: P-1 grilled+user-gated 2026-06-11 (docs/decisions/PROPOSALS.md). publishLibrary emits index.html (library) + {slug}/index.html (full heads projection, all readings) + sitemap.txt. Citation = durable ref {slug}/index.html#note-<logicalId> beside the interactive viewer deep-link; both minted from logicalId. Note bodies render via the SAME snarkdown+DOMPurify pipeline the Viewer uses (injected; escape fallback in non-DOM contexts) — sanitization policy cannot drift; CONTEXT 232 gate satisfied-by-reuse. Rationale: user-published repos previously had ZERO human-readable surface; archives could not capture the scholarly words; hash routes are not relitigated. Rides with site.ts hotspot cleanup. ADR-0014.

title: Q-2 — Readings: membership exclusivity (Q5) split from display exclusivity — Studio rail composes, Viewer stays exclusive in v1
rationale: P-2 grilled+user-gated 2026-06-11 (docs/decisions/PROPOSALS.md). Q5 membership untouched (one Note -> one Reading, IIIF AnnotationPage model). Display is surface-local: Studio rail = visible SET (toggles) + active reading as explicit pen-radio (drawing files there; never inferred from visibility); comparing (2+ visible) renders ALL marks outline-only, stroke=identity, solo-on-hover restores one fill; rail=use, ReadingsModal=edit, readingFilter dropdown RETIRED (one entry point). Viewer v1 stays exclusive radio (CONTEXT 173); opt-in compare = v1.1 beside the Compare layout. Margin cards scope with visibility (same projection as canvas). Authoring/reading asymmetry recorded as v1.1 forcing function.

title: Q-3 — Viewer reads the working store live — one canonical store, two same-origin apps; Publish = durability only
rationale: User gate 2026-06-11. A newly created exhibit appears in the co-deployed Viewer WITHOUT a publish/import step: Studio and Viewer are two same-origin apps over ONE canonical store (the Q-2 three-configs store, OPFS for unbound). Viewer probes the working store on load and merges local exhibits into the hall alongside hosted ones. Mechanism: OPFS is per-origin; GH Pages co-deploy (/Archie/studio/ + /Archie/viewer/) is already same-origin, and local dev gets a single-origin proxy mirroring the GH Pages path layout (/studio/, /viewer/) — which also makes dev mirror prod base paths. 'Publish' is therefore NOT how exhibits become visible to the author — it is reserved for durability/visibility-to-others: bake the static tree (durable anchors ADR-0014, exhibits.json, archival HTML), commit/push. User-facing badge language: 'Local — only you can see this, in this browser' vs 'Published — on the web, citable'. Liveness v1 = on Viewer load (no BroadcastChannel). Cross-origin deployments degrade gracefully: OPFS probe fails → exactly today's hosted/portable behavior; live mode must never be a hard dependency. Implementation seam: the OPFS working-store format (library.json + exhibits/{slug}/annotations/) differs from the published format loadLibrary reads — Studio's store-reader moves into render-core so the Viewer can consume it through the existing source-agnostic read seam (ADR-0010, same ExhibitView as portable mode).

title: Q-2 — Section also serializes as a supplementing WADM annotation (additive; Range stays canonical)
rationale: Each Section ships twice: the canonical IIIF Range in structures[] (Archie reads it) PLUS a derived 'supplementing' WADM annotation in {slug}/annotations/narrative.json (one ordered AnnotationCollection), so pure-WADM/IIIF annotation tools that read AnnotationPages — not Ranges — can consume the narrative. Range affordances baked in: title->IIIF label, region->FragmentSelector target, prose->describing TextualBody, order->archie:order; each Range links via supplementary (IIIF Pres 3 sec 5.4). Carries NO archie DAG fields, so the reload importer ignores it (no double-count). Non-invasive: Section model, read path, and Range serialization unchanged. ADR-0017; verified 593 render-core tests + end-to-end bake.

title: Q-8 — Published pages emit og-tags/JSON-LD/sitemap.xml, extending self-describing artifact
rationale: Phase A reach work: static-pages.ts pageShell() also injects OpenGraph + Twitter meta, schema.org JSON-LD (CreativeWork/ImageObject), canonical link, and upgrades sitemap.txt to sitemap.xml. Extends Q-2 (self-describing artifact, mx-29f1df) and ADR-0014. Reuses ogImageFor() from apps/viewer/src/og-image.ts. Build-time only, zero runtime cost.

title: Q-9 — In-browser tiling is gated on a bundle-budget + OffscreenCanvas-DZI feasibility go/no-go spike
rationale: BLOCKER 4 / axis 17. Before building the OffscreenCanvas DZI slicer, a [SPIKE] must (a) measure the real published viewer bundle vs the 240KB budget (axis 16, currently unmeasured), and (b) prototype tile-slice generation against one large (>=8k px) image. GO requires: slicer produces OSD-readable DZI in-browser AND bundle delta stays within an agreed cap. NO-GO parks tiling at v1.2 with the single-responsive-JPEG path (bake.ts) unchanged.

title: Q-3 — Within-exhibit search is a client-side static minisearch index, no server
rationale: Axis 11. Search + tag-filter over note bodies/tags is built into the published Viewer as a client-side minisearch index (consistent with backend-less architecture). Index built from the same annotation tree Reader.svelte renders; tagsOfAnnotation() already exists in render-core. No new publish-time server dependency.

title: Q-4 — Within-exhibit discovery is a mode-independent finder overlay, not a list filter
rationale: Grilled 2026-06-20. Search+tags = one overlay at ExhibitView level (works in grid AND narrative; narrative has no note list). Scope: ALL readings — selecting a result switches activeReading via the existing #/<slug>/a/<noteId> arrival path (ExhibitView:76). Tag chips clickable everywhere (grid cards + narrative notes) -> open overlay pre-scoped to that tag. Combination: tags OR each other (additive), text query ANDs the union. Overlay is a PURE FINDER: canvas only changes on result-select (reuses fitBounds), no persistent canvas-filter, no URL state (transient like readings). Trigger: visible affordance in ExhibitView chrome + Cmd/Ctrl-K accelerator. Supersedes the plan's original 'tag-filter in Reader.svelte note list' framing. Extends Q-3 (client-side minisearch).

title: Q-5 — Keyboard surface for regions is the linear index, not the canvas markers
rationale: Grilled 2026-06-20. Each note exists twice (SVG marker + index entry). The INDEX is the focusable/keyboard-operable surface: note cards (grid) or section spine (narrative), both already wired to drive the canvas via initialSelected->fitBounds. Markers get role/aria-label for SR but tabindex=-1 (out of Tab order) — avoids double-stops and tabbing to off-screen markers at zoom. Annotorious renders markers as SVG DOM (.a9s-annotation, Reader.svelte:246), so labeling is feasible. Same principle both modes.

title: Q-10 — Tiling gate is about WEB in-browser generation only, not tiling-as-a-feature (reframes Q-9)
rationale: Grilled 2026-06-20. Desktop Tauri target (digital.compost.archie) can generate tiles natively via Rust vips, and external IIIF already serves tiles — all fill the same additive optional AObject.tileSource?: TileSourceDescriptor (zero migration; existing exhibits have no tileSource). So the Q-9 go/no-go decides only whether the OffscreenCanvas WEB generator is worth building, not whether tiling exists. Spike still prototypes producing a TileSourceDescriptor so the data contract is validated even on a web NO-GO.

title: Q-11 — Tiling gate = GO: build web OffscreenCanvas DZI slicer, capped off the load-time island
rationale: Task 6 gate (2026-06-20), from spike docs/spikes/2026-06-tiling-feasibility.md. Measured: ExhibitView island 282.4KB gz (OSD+Annotorious, already ~42KB over the 240KB axis-16 budget — a SEPARATE pre-existing ticket, not tiling). DZI math verified (14 levels/1033 tiles @8000x6000). OffscreenCanvas encode feasible (~180-200MB transient, worker-recommended, author-side). Slicer delta <=3KB gz, ~0 on island. VERDICT GO with CAP: Task 7 adds <=5KB gz to any viewer-loaded chunk, target 0 on ExhibitView (slicer in worker/studio bake chunk). RESIDUAL: actual pixel encode + OSD-render needs browser verification (DZI is OSD-native format, low risk). Desktop-Rust/IIIF remain valid descriptor fillers (Q-10).
